name: Aust-Agder (Norway, 1958–2019)

colors.symbolism: gold (Or), from the historic Raabyggjelaget seal

description: Five equal horizontal stripes alternating red and gold, beginning with red at the top.
history: Granted to Aust-Agder county on 14 February 1958. The design (barry of five gules and Or) is taken from the medieval seal of Raabyggjelaget. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Vest-Agder into the new Agder county.

features.symbolism: five barry stripes red and gold, derived from a medieval seal of Raabyggjelaget, the historic district covering inner Aust-Agder
